strengths: Demonstrated backend and distributed-systems expertise through work at Dunnhumby with microservices infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests per second.
Experience with event-driven platforms (GISCollective) and AI-agent infrastructure (re:cinq), showing familiarity with modular systems and evolving workflows.
Track record of scaling engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development at RIDE Capital) and contributing to production systems in high-stakes environments.
concerns: No explicit experience with multi-agent AI systems in production fintech contexts; prior AI work appears limited to MCP tools and developer tooling rather than commercial agentic AI.
No evidence of fintech-infrastructure familiarity (e.g., credit systems, payment flows, or embedded finance) despite the role's heavy emphasis on this domain.
Career trajectory shows breadth across many domains (GIS, embedded systems, web tools) but lacks deep, sustained specialization in AI-native fintech architecture or leadership at scale.
response.justification: Bogdan’s resume emphasizes breadth over depth in AI and fintech, with no clear record of designing multi-agent systems for high-stakes commercial environments or building AI-native fintech infrastructure (e.g., real-time credit risk, working-capital management). His engineering leadership, while rigorous, has focused on general software practices rather than scaling AI-driven systems or leading hybrid engineering models in regulated domains. The absence of fintech-specific expertise and proven agentic-AI architecture work undermines his fit for a CTO role at a venture-backed fintech scale-up.
